class.name: RuntimePlacementController3D
class.brief_description: Runtime controller for selecting, moving, rotating, and flipping placed 3D objects.
class.description:
[RuntimePlacementController3D] watches a target container, projects pointer input through a [Camera3D], and draws an outline plus rotation handle around the selected child [Node3D].
It is designed for real projects that already have GLTF/GLB content, without requiring the sample scene.
Use the API directly from your own game logic: 1. Configure [member camera_path] and [member targets_root_path]. 2. Call [method select_target_at_screen_position] for tap selection if needed. 3. Use [method move_selected_to_screen_position], [method rotate_selected], [method flip_selected_x], [method flip_selected_z]. 4. Use [method place_target_on_floor] when spawning GLTF/GLB instances. 5. Control behavior with [member require_double_tap_to_move], [member clear_selection_on_empty_tap], [member move_guides_enabled], [member drag_bounds], and [member drag_bounds_enabled].
For engine-level integration in existing projects, explicit boundary APIs are also available: [method create_boundary], [method update_boundary], [method update_color], [method delete_boundary].
